text: The uniportal bath surgery was done through a left 4 cm incision at the anterior axillary line and a big solid mass about 4 by 12 cm was found in the left posterior mediastinum.
the dissection was started at the posterior lateral aspect of the mass
clipping and dividing of some intercostal vessels then the inferior aspect of the mass was dissected
the mask then dissected from the wall of the descending aorta there was no involvement of the element of the aorta in this case
An indoor bag was used to take the mass out of the pleural space.
An arthroscopic guided intercostal block was done.
Then a 24 French chest drain was inserted through the same incision and then the wound was closed in layers as usual.
The post-operative course was uneventful and the chest drain removal was done 24 hours after the surgery and the patient is charged home three days after the surgery.
